project.name: NYX
project.description: AI-powered crypto learning platform that helps beginners become confident traders

codeSync.description: Automatic two-way sync of code changes between GitHub and Lovable

issuesSync.description: GitHub Issues are not automatically synced to Lovable. Use GitHub issue templates to reference Lovable tasks and vice versa.

issues.lovableReference: Link related Lovable task URL in GitHub issue description
issues.githubReference: Link GitHub issue URL in Lovable task comments

pullRequests.template:
- **Related Issue(s)**: Link to GitHub issues
- **Related Lovable Task(s)**: Link to Lovable tasks
- **Description**: Brief summary of changes
- **Testing**: How to test these changes

bestPractices: Always connect to Lovable project settings first before making changes
Make code changes in Lovable and let sync push to GitHub
Do not rename or delete the GitHub repository after connecting
Document related Lovable tasks in GitHub issue descriptions using full URLs
Document related GitHub issues in Lovable task comments using full URLs
Use consistent naming conventions for cross-repository tracking
Keep the main branch as the single source of truth for code

setup.step1: Go to Lovable project settings for 'nyx'
setup.step5: Enable two-way sync on the main branch
setup.step6: Verify sync is active by checking recent commits

troubleshooting.syncBroken: Sync breaks if repository is renamed or deleted. Create a new project in Lovable and reconnect.
troubleshooting.issuesNotSyncing: GitHub Issues are not natively synced. Use cross-referencing strategy instead (link URLs in issue/task descriptions).
troubleshooting.conflictResolution: In case of conflicts, Lovable version takes precedence for code sync. Resolve manually in GitHub.
